title: Does Anyone Else Find Evangelion VOX Hilarious
display_title: Does Anyone Else Find Evangelion VOX Hilarious
wikitext:
[]: Does Anyone Else Find Evangelion: VOX Hilarious? Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 10, 2007 2:40 am I mean, it's rap that they're trying to somehow tie to Evangelion. For example, these lyrics from the track "Armageddon", a rap song to the tune of Pachelbel's Canon crack me up in particular: "Catholic, Muslim... There's too many religions!" "You don't even stop to think/that what happened to the dinosaurs could make us all extinct!" And the opening track... erm... with... with the preacher... "EEEEEEEEVAAAAAAAAAANGELION!!! WORLD'S WORST NIGHTMARE!" And, of course, "X-plicit"... erm... what? A bunch of swearing with the audio of Shinji masturbating in the background, then some sort of strange remix of Asuka saying "Anta baka?" and the such. Conclusion: For me, impossible to take seriously. The only original content of any merit other than giggles, in my opinion, is the KST: Tumbling Down mix. After all, it was the last track that Nina Simone recorded before her death.

[]: Post by slothen » Sun Jun 10, 2007 10:22 am i have absolutely no idea what you're talking about.

[]: Post by drinian » Sun Jun 10, 2007 10:48 am slothen wrote: i have absolutely no idea what you're talking about.

[]: Post by Executor » Sun Jun 10, 2007 11:13 am Wait a minute, they had an Evangelion rap album? *hit's up google* ... Holy fuck... I think I died a little by see that.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 10, 2007 12:28 pm Apparently VOX is a little more obscure than I thought it was. Still, you didn't really expect them to miss the opportunity to milk the rap market, did you? (Erm... milking a market... I should have said "rap cow", or possibly "rapping tomato".)

[]: Post by n00dle » Sun Jun 10, 2007 1:28 pm Indeed, it is quite embarrassing. I still have it though cause I'm a completionist and like to have as many Eva related albums as possible. Anyway, at least a couple of the tracks aren't total crap.

[]: Post by Tokpile Quohog » Sun Jun 10, 2007 1:31 pm And I thought that the character select music in SF:third-strike was bad.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jun 10, 2007 2:28 pm I acutally kinda like the Kanon D Dur rap, only thing I've heard from VOX. ^^; In terms of this "EEEEEEEEVAAAAAAAAAANGELION!!! WORLD'S WORST NIGHTMARE!", on S2 Works and Symphony, there's a variation of Borderline Case with chorus (2 different takes on S2 Works disc 7) that has those lyrics. It's nicely done actually. I don't like Rap or R&B (thought Thanatos~If I Can't Be Yours~ is made of win! ), so yeah. Good thing I've never listened to it. It sounds painful. And, of course, "X-plicit"... erm... what? A bunch of swearing with the audio of Shinji masturbating in the background, then some sort of strange remix of Asuka saying "Anta baka?" and the such.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 10, 2007 4:23 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: WTF?! That piqued my interest! :shock: So...is Tumbling Down Remix any good, then? Is it download *coughs* worthy?

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jun 10, 2007 4:40 pm WTF?!?! That was beyond weird, especially xplicit. ^^; (Oh, and yes, I LOLED. ) Power Rangers, huh? We kickin' it Old Skool. EDIT: Who's singing the Tumbling Down Remix?? It sounds like Loren from Loren & Mash.

[]: Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun Jun 10, 2007 4:42 pm Biggest waste of $8.00 on a bootleg EVAR.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 10, 2007 4:53 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: Biggest waste of $8.00 on a bootleg EVAR.

[]: Post by n00dle » Sun Jun 10, 2007 5:05 pm Seriously. Someone similar to, but legally distinct from me downloaded every Eva album ever made in one massive torrent. Not that I condone doing any such thing. That would be wrong. Very wrong... *do it! do it now! stick it to the man!*

[]: Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 10, 2007 5:11 pm n00dle wrote: Someone similar to, but legally distinct from me

[]: Post by marmotgirl » Sun Jun 10, 2007 5:25 pm I propose that from now on, every time someone whines about EVA@School, Angelic Days, Chromosome-XX or whatever This Week's Rediculous "Series-Demeaning" Eva Crap happens to be, we direct them to this monstrosity.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jun 10, 2007 5:36 pm NAveryW wrote: I'm hoping it's okay to post these here, as it's really just a small sample of what's on this disc that nobody really knows about anyway, plus there are Evangelion songs available for download on Eva Monkey Radio.

[]: Post by slothen » Sun Jun 10, 2007 5:45 pm You can't delete MEMORIES, Eva Monkey.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 10, 2007 5:59 pm slothen wrote: You can't delete MEMORIES, Eva Monkey.

[]: Post by n00dle » Sun Jun 10, 2007 6:42 pm I can neither confirm nor deny the Lollipop Guild's involvement with illegal activities.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 10, 2007 6:56 pm n00dle wrote: I can neither confirm nor deny the Lollipop Guild's involvement with illegal activities.

[]: Does Anyone Else Find Evangelion: VOX Hilarious? Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 10, 2007 2:40 am I mean, it's rap that they're trying to somehow tie to Evangelion. For example, these lyrics from the track "Armageddon", a rap song to the tune of Pachelbel's Canon crack me up in particular: "Catholic, Muslim... There's too many religions!" "You don't even stop to think/that what happened to the dinosaurs could make us all extinct!" And the opening track... erm... with... with the preacher... "EEEEEEEEVAAAAAAAAAANGELION!!! WORLD'S WORST NIGHTMARE!" And, of course, "X-plicit"... erm... what? A bunch of swearing with the audio of Shinji masturbating in the background, then some sort of strange remix of Asuka saying "Anta baka?" and the such. Conclusion: For me, impossible to take seriously. The only original content of any merit other than giggles, in my opinion, is the KST: Tumbling Down mix. After all, it was the last track that Nina Simone recorded before her death.
